paper_title: Stabilizing Reinforcement Learning with LLMs: Formulation and Practices
research_focus: Reinforcement Learning with Large Language Models
key_problem: Mismatch between sequence-level rewards and token-level optimization objectives in RL with LLMs, leading to training stability issues
proposed_solution: Novel formulation using surrogate token-level objective as first-order approximation of sequence-level reward
key_insights: Surrogate token-level objective is valid when training-inference discrepancy and policy staleness are minimized
Importance sampling weight is inherent component of surrogate token-level objective
Routing Replay approach reduces both training-inference discrepancy and policy staleness for MoE models

methodology.computational_resources: Hundreds of thousands of GPU hours

key_findings: For on-policy training, basic policy gradient algorithm with importance sampling correction yields highest training stability
Off-policy updates can accelerate convergence but require careful combination with other techniques

definitions.stable_training: Training process where model performance steadily improves and internal state evolves smoothly
definitions.on_policy: Rollout policy identical to target policy
definitions.off_policy: Rollout policy different from target policy

title: Stabilizing Reinforcement Learning with LLMs: Formulation and Practices
